sections.paragraph:
συμβαίνω impf. συνέβαινον; fut. συμβήσομαι; 2 aor. συνέβην; pf. συμβέβηκα (Aeschyl., Hdt.+)

① to join someone in going somewhere, go along with of Paul who went on shipboard with others AcPl Ha 5, 17 (cp. 5, 15 ἀνέβη).

② to occur as event or process, happen, come about (Trag., Hdt. +) συμβαίνει τί τινι (Trag., Thu. et al.; TestSim 2:13) Mk 10:32 (w. ref. to death: last will and testament of Aristot. in Diog. L. 5, 11; 12); Ac 20:19; 1 Cor 10:11; 1 Pt 4:12; 2 Pt 2:22; 1 Cl 23:3 (scripture quot. of unknown origin); B 19:6; D 3:10. οὕτως συμβαίνει πᾶσι Hm 5, 2, 7. οὕτως κἀμοὶ συνέβη the same thing has happened to me GJs 13:1. καθὼς φρονοῦσιν καὶ συμβήσεται αὐτοῖς in accordance w. their opinions, so the outcome shall be for them ISm 2. W. inf. foll. (Hdt. 6, 103 al.; ins; POxy 491, 10; Ar. 8, 6; Just., D. 23, 1) Hv 3, 7, 6. συνέβη foll. by acc. and inf. (Hdt. 7, 166 al.; SIG 535, 5; 685, 36f; 1 Esdr 1:23; Jos., Ant. 9, 185; Mel., HE 4, 26, 9; Ath. 5, 1) συνέβη βαστάζεσθαι αὐτὸν ὑπὸ τῶν στρατιωτῶν Ac 21:35 (B-D-F §393, 5; 408; Rob. 392; 1043); εἰς οὐδὲν δὲ συνέβη τελευτῆσαι (τελέσαι v.l.) τὴν τάξιν αὐτῶν but it came to pass that finally their (the angels’) status was terminated Papias (4).—τὸ συμβεβηκός τινι what has happened to someone (Sus 26 Theod.; TestJob 1:6 al. [pl.]; Jos., Vi. 51; Just., D. 78, 8) Ac 3:10. Sing., without the dat. τὸ συμβάν what had happened (Epict. 3, 24, 13; Appian, Hann. 36 §154; TestSol 1:4 [pl.]; Agatharchides: 86 Fgm. 20, 211 Jac. [in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 211]; cp. Jos., Ant. 13, 413 τὰ ξυμβάντα) GPt 14:59. Pl. τὰ συμβεβηκότα the things that had happened (Isocr. 5, 18; 1 Macc 4:26; EpArist; Jos., Bell. 4, 43, Ant. 13, 194) Lk 24:14.—M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

acc. acc. = accusative

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

  1. pl. = plural

dat. dat. = dative

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Agatharchides Agatharchides , of Cnidus, II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 956
